title: Homelab network inventory import
description: Schema for files accepted by the Import button in network-inventory.html. The loader (normalizeData / normalizeNetwork) is intentionally permissive: missing fields are filled with defaults rather than rejected, so this schema documents the shape but does NOT use additionalProperties: false. UNKNOWN FIELDS ARE DROPPED ON IMPORT — the normalizer constructs each record from a fixed key list, so fields not enumerated in this schema (or in normalizeNetwork) are silently discarded on round-trip. External producers that add custom annotations should not expect them to survive an import → export cycle. Storage key in localStorage: homenet.inventory.v1. The top-level schemaVersion is required and must equal 0 (frozen during in-development phase); loaders refuse anything else rather than attempting to convert. KEEP IN SYNC: when network-inventory.html adds, removes, or renames a field on any record (network / vlan / host / interface / service / credential) or changes an enum constant, update this schema in the same change. Files may also be wrapped in an encrypted envelope (see EncryptedEnvelope) — the loader unwraps after a passphrase prompt and processes the inner `data` block through the same path. NOTE: $id is intentionally absent — the schema has no canonical public URL, so external tooling must reference it by local path rather than URL.

MultiNetworkExport.description: Full backup shape produced by Export → 'All networks'. Importing this REPLACES all current networks (after a confirm).

schemaVersion.description: Format version. Loaders reject anything other than 0. Frozen at 0 during in-development phase.

networks.description: Map of networkId → network. networkId is a slug-style stable key.

activeNetworkId.description: Key into networks. If missing or unknown, the loader picks the first network.

SingleNetwork.description: Single-network shape produced by Export → 'Current network only'. On import, the user is offered 'add as new network' (recommended) or 'replace current network'.

schemaVersion.description: Format version. Loaders reject anything other than 0. Frozen at 0 during in-development phase.

EncryptedEnvelope.description: Encrypted-file wrapper. Produced either by the offline encrypt_hosts.py tool or by the in-app exporter when running in an unlocked encrypted session — both emit the same shape, so the file is symmetric on round-trip. The loader detects the envelope by the presence of top-level `kdf` and prompts for a passphrase. After unlock, `data` is processed through the same import path as a plaintext file (multi vs single sniff). Encrypted fields (currently only credential.password) appear as {iv, ct} blobs inside `data`; a partially-encrypted state — where some passwords are plaintext strings and others are blobs — is allowed and round-trips as-is, since blanks set during in-app editing stay plaintext rather than encrypting an empty string. The envelope itself is also persisted to localStorage under `homenet.inventory.envelope.v1` so the in-app session can re-unlock on reload and re-emit a matching envelope on Export. SCHEMA_VERSION inside `data.schemaVersion` is still required and must equal 0.

kdf.description: PBKDF2 parameters used to derive the AES-GCM key from the passphrase.

cipher.description: Symmetric cipher used for the per-value blobs.

encrypted_fields.description: Documentation-only list of dotted field paths whose values are EncryptedBlob shapes inside `data`. The runtime loader does NOT consult this list — encrypted blobs are detected structurally. In v1 this is typically ["credentials.*.password"].

data.description: Inner inventory shape — same as the plaintext top-level (multi-network export or single-network export).

Network.description: One network's full inventory. All four child maps are optional; the loader fills in empty objects.

network.description: Network-level metadata. Both fields default to empty string.

vlans.description: Map of vlanId → VLAN. vlanId is the user-facing stable key (e.g. 'VLAN10').

credentials.description: Map of credentialId → Credential. Referenced by host.credential_ref and service.credential_ref.

tag.description: 802.1Q VLAN tag. Required and used for sort order.

gateway_ip.description: Gateway IPv4 address (no prefix), e.g. '192.168.10.1'. Empty when there is no gateway.

subnet_prefix.description: Subnet prefix length (CIDR mask). Null/missing when there is no gateway or the prefix is unknown.

dhcp.description: Whether DHCP is enabled on this VLAN.

dhcp_range_start.description: First address of the DHCP pool (inclusive), e.g. '192.168.10.100'. Validated as an IPv4 address at edit time but not on import. Empty when unspecified or when dhcp is false.

dhcp_range_end.description: Last address of the DHCP pool (inclusive), e.g. '192.168.10.200'. Validated as an IPv4 address at edit time but not on import. Empty when unspecified or when dhcp is false.

display_name.description: Human-readable name. Should NOT include parenthetical disambiguators like '(VM 100 on bockscar)' — those are composed at render time from vm_id + hosted_on.

items.description: Alternate hostnames only — no colons. Use identifiers[] for non-hostname IDs.

identifiers.description: Free-floating identifiers that don't belong to an interface (Bluetooth MACs, serial numbers, IMEIs, ...).

hosted_on.description: hostId of the parent host (typically used when kind = virtual or container).

vm_id.description: Hypervisor-side VM/instance identifier (e.g. Proxmox VMID '100'). Stored as a string because the values in use are arbitrary tokens. Most meaningful when kind = 'virtual'; ignored otherwise.

manufacturer.description: Hardware vendor (e.g. 'Dell', 'Protectli', 'Google'). Combined with model at render time.

model.description: Product model name only — vendor lives in 'manufacturer'.

hardware_revision.description: Optional revision/spin identifier (e.g. 'Rev A2', 'Backplate-5.26'). Stored only; not currently rendered.

management_vlan.description: vlanId reference. Not enforced — dangling refs are tolerated.

credential_ref.description: credentialId reference. Not enforced — dangling refs are tolerated.

links.description: External URLs associated with this host (vendor cloud, dashboard, documentation). Distinct from service.url, which points at a service listening on the host itself.

peripherals.description: Physical peripherals attached to or passed through to this host (USB sticks, PCIe cards). Distinct from interfaces, which model network connectivity.

Software.description: Structured software/firmware descriptor. The renderer composes a display string roughly as 'name (edition, version, kernel ...)'. For switches/APs use 'name' for the firmware family (e.g. 'GS308E firmware') and 'version' for the build number; software.name being empty hides the chip entirely.

kernel.description: Kernel version where applicable (e.g. '6.1.21-v8+'). Empty for firmware/appliance-style entries.

edition.description: Edition / spin / base distro (e.g. 'Community Edition', 'Debian 11 bullseye').

mac.description: MAC address of this interface in colon-separated hex (e.g. 'aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff'). Empty string is allowed and means 'unset'. The loader does not enforce the pattern, but the schema rejects malformed non-empty values for external validators.

parent.description: Name of the parent interface (used for virtual/sub-interfaces).

access_vlan.description: vlanId for access mode. Empty when mode = trunk.

native_vlan.description: vlanId for the native (untagged / PVID) VLAN on a trunk. Empty when mode = access, or when the trunk has no useful native VLAN.

allowed_vlans.description: vlanId list for trunk mode. Empty when mode = access.

ip_addresses.description: List of IPv4 addresses bound to this interface, each as { address, prefix }.

IpAddress.description: An IPv4 address with optional prefix length. Form input still accepts the full 'a.b.c.d/m' string for ergonomics; it is parsed into { address, prefix } on save.

ConnectedTo.description: Structured pointer to the remote endpoint of the cable/link. All sub-fields optional; missing or empty host_id means 'not connected'. via_bridge names a Linux bridge, OVS bridge, or virtual switch (e.g. Proxmox 'vmbr0'); via_tag is the 802.1Q tag the link delivers (1–4094) when relevant.

host_id.description: Remote hostId. Not enforced — dangling refs are tolerated.

interface.description: Remote interface name (must match a name on the remote host's interfaces).

via_bridge.description: Bridge or virtual switch carrying the link.

via_tag.description: VLAN tag the link delivers, when relevant. Null/missing if not applicable.

id.description: Stable per-host identifier for the service. Generated by slugify(name) with collision suffix on import; readonly in the edit form. Allows other records (e.g. credentials) to refer to a service even when its name changes.

port.description: Port number. Stored as either a number or a string (the form input is text). Empty string = unspecified.

Link.description: A labeled external URL on a host. Use for vendor portals, cloud dashboards, or doc links — anything reachable from a browser that isn't a service running on the host. The label is what the UI renders; the URL is opened on click.

label.description: Human-readable name for the link (e.g. 'Nest cloud', 'Vendor docs').

url.description: Full URL including scheme. Not validated on import.

Peripheral.description: A physical peripheral attached to or passed through to this host. Slot is the host-side index; usb_vid_pid is the USB vendor:product ID; description is free-form (vendor / product / role).

slot.description: Bare slot identifier (e.g. '0', '1', '2'). Do NOT include the kind prefix — that's redundant with `kind`.

usb_vid_pid.description: USB vendor:product ID in lsusb format (e.g. '1d6b:0002'). Only meaningful when `kind` is `usb`.

Identifier.description: A free-floating identifier on a host that doesn't belong to a specific interface — e.g. a Bluetooth MAC, device serial, IMEI. Renders as '${label || kind}: ${value}'.

value.description: The identifier value (e.g. a MAC address, serial number, IMEI).

label.description: Optional human-readable label shown instead of kind in the chip (e.g. 'bluetooth').

Credential.description: Plaintext password storage is supported but discouraged; external_ref is the intended field for pointing at an external vault entry. It carries a provider name (e.g. 'Bitwarden', '1Password') and a provider-scoped id (vault item id, path, etc.) — rendered as 'provider: id'. The reverse linkage to hosts/services is NOT stored on the credential — it is derived from host.credential_ref / service.credential_ref. The credential edit modal exposes that reverse view for convenience and writes back to the host/service side on save.

password.description: Plaintext password OR per-value encrypted blob. When the file is loaded from an encrypted envelope, this field carries the {iv, ct} shape and is decrypted on demand. When the file is plaintext, OR the field has been re-saved in a plaintext session, OR an empty password was set in an encrypted session (the empty string is not a secret — never encrypted), this is a plain string. When a non-empty password is created or edited in an unlocked encrypted session, the in-app save handler re-encrypts it with a fresh IV before assigning, so the field flips back to the {iv, ct} shape. The loader accepts either; unknown shapes collapse to empty string. Mixed states inside one credentials map (some entries plaintext, some blobs) are explicitly supported and round-trip through Import/Export.

external_ref.description: Pointer to an external secret store. Both fields optional; provider is the vault/manager name, id is the provider-scoped identifier (e.g. Bitwarden item id, vault path). Display format is '${provider}: ${id}' when both are present.

EncryptedBlob.description: Per-value encrypted secret. Two base64-encoded fields: iv (AES-GCM nonce) and ct (ciphertext including AES-GCM auth tag). Produced by the offline encrypt_hosts.py tool OR by the in-app save handler (Crypto.encryptField) when a credential is created/edited in an unlocked encrypted session; the loader detects it structurally (object with exactly these two string keys). Plaintext stays as a plain string in the same field — the field accepts either shape. Every encryption call uses a fresh 12-byte random IV; AES-GCM nonce reuse with the same key is catastrophic, so producers MUST NOT reuse IVs.

IdString.description: Stable record key. Readonly once created. Must start with an alphanumeric and contain only alphanumerics, hyphens, and underscores. The app generates IDs as opaque random strings of the form `<typetag>_<6 hex chars>` — `n_` for networks, `v_` for vlans, `h_` for hosts, `c_` for credentials, `s_` for services — so IDs carry no semantic content and the dict-key channel doesn't leak identifying info even when the file is plaintext on disk. Older fixtures and imported files may use semantic ids (e.g. 'router_01', 'VLAN10'); the loader accepts those and the pattern still matches, but newly created records are always opaque. Mixed case is allowed for backwards compatibility.

HostKind.description: HOST_KINDS in network-inventory.html. Default on import: 'physical'.

HostRole.description: HOST_ROLES in network-inventory.html. Default on import: 'other'.

InterfaceType.description: INTERFACE_TYPES in network-inventory.html. Default on import: 'ethernet'.

InterfaceMode.description: INTERFACE_MODES in network-inventory.html. Default on import: 'access'.

ServiceCategory.description: SERVICE_CATEGORIES in network-inventory.html. Default on import: 'application'. Drives the Services tab chip filter.

ServiceProtocol.description: PROTOCOLS in network-inventory.html. Default on import: 'https'.

CredentialType.description: CREDENTIAL_TYPES in network-inventory.html. Indicates where the credential is used: shell/SSH, web UI, API, console (serial/IPMI), SNMP, database, or other. Default on import: 'shell'.

PeripheralKind.description: PERIPHERAL_KINDS in network-inventory.html. Default on import: 'usb'.

IdentifierKind.description: IDENTIFIER_KINDS in network-inventory.html. Default on import: 'other'.
